title: 控方证人
summary:
On an underground platform, a man loses his balance and falls toward an approaching train. Despite his intense fear of death, Hammel decides to sacrifice himself to save the man. He uses his remaining strength to pull the man back onto the platform, but Hammel falls onto the tracks himself. As he dies, he experiences a sense of freedom and hears the sound of Pan's flute and the flapping of wings.

Later, Lor Dobler, a thirty-two-year-old French engineer, crosses the Seine River to visit an apartment in the Caudinet. He is greeted by an elderly woman named Elise (also referred to as Eileen or Eleanor), who is a devout Catholic. Upon his arrival, Elise expresses her disapproval of the séances conducted by her mistress, Mademoiselle Simonne, claiming the practice is a deal with the devil that is making the lady pale, weak, and prone to headaches.

Raul (referred to as Lor Dobler earlier) attempts to soothe Elise, explaining that once he marries Simonne, the séances will cease. He defends Simonne’s character, describing her as the best medium in France and an honest person rather than a con artist. However, Elise remains fearful, suggesting that mediumship goes against nature and that the souls being contacted might not want to let Simonne go. The tension is interrupted by the arrival of Simonne, a tall, blonde, and elegant woman. Raul greets her affectionately, noting her extreme paleness and questioning her health.
